conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or young people under 20 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child - Alex, who is 15 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand your residency status.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here (for example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another qualifying immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester. And yes, I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to ask one more important question to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care (such as in foster care or a children's home)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually Alex is fostered - I'm fostering them. But I don't get anything from the council for their maintenance or accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important detail. Let me ask a follow-up question to ensure I have the complete picture.

When you say you don't get anything from the council for Alex's maintenance or accommodation, does that mean you receive absolutely no payments or support from the local authority in relation to fostering Alex? And are you fostering Alex through a formal arrangement with the local authority, or is this an informal private fostering arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I receive absolutely no payments from the local authority for Alex. It's a formal fostering arrangement through the council, but they're not paying me anything for their accommodation or maintenance. I'm covering all the costs myself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility.

Let me carefully consider your situation against the Child Benefit rules:

- You have a child (Alex, age 15) who lives with you
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- Alex is in your care through a formal fostering arrangement
- Crucially, you receive no payments from the local authority for Alex's maintenance or accommodation

According to the Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child you're fostering, but only if the local authority does not pay anything towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. Since you've confirmed that you receive absolutely no payments from the council and are covering all costs yourself, you meet this requirement.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 15 years old and lives with you
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- Although Alex is fostered through a formal local authority arrangement, you receive no financial payments from the council for their accommodation or maintenance

The key factor here is that you're not receiving any payments from the local authority. Under Child Benefit rules, foster carers can claim Child Benefit if the local authority does not pay anything towards the child's accommodation or maintenance - which applies in your case since you're covering all costs yourself.

You should be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant has the right to reside in the UK. Although Alex is fostered through a formal local authority arrangement, the claimant receives no payments from the local authority for Alex's accommodation or maintenance. According to Child Benefit rules, you can claim for a child you foster only if the local authority does not pay anything towards the child's accommodation or maintenance. Since no payments are received, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. All core eligibility criteria are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant has the right to reside in the UK. The fostering situation does not disqualify the claim because the local authority provides no financial support for Alex's accommodation or maintenance, which is the key exception that allows foster carers to claim Child Benefit.
